name: qa-planner
description: Portable QA planning plugin for converting requirements into planning_state JSON, test plans, test cases, coverage maps, review feedback, and downstream handoff contracts, with conditional Plane MCP and Notion MCP output integration.

interface.shortDescription: Create QA plans from requirements, Plane, or Notion.
interface.longDescription: QA Planner provides portable QA planning for normalizing requirements, resolving blocking ambiguity and conflicting sources without unsupported assumptions, creating deterministic planning_state JSON, rendering test plans, coverage maps, and test cases from the Template Test Case field model, managing OK/NOK and partial review loops, handling large inputs incrementally, and producing governed handoff contracts for qa-executor, qa-automation, and qa-reporter. Plane.so MCP integration is documented as a conditional reference loaded only for Plane inputs. Notion MCP integration is documented as a conditional reference loaded only for Notion outputs and creates a test plan page plus a mandatory test case database when database tooling is available.
interface.defaultPrompt: Use qa-planner to create a QA planning package from requirements, a Plane work item, or a Notion output request.
